trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: 'It's hard to keep the peace if you can't even control your temper.' [Scryfall] The quote is unattributed. The irony is mechanical: the card pumps a creature into a rage (+2/+0 + haste) precisely to get it killed this turn, and the 'reward' for the creature dying is that the law (a white-blue Detective token) shows up. The flavor text's admonition is the punchline — rage ends in investigation.

Felonious Rage costs {R} and creates a conditional Detective token: if the boosted creature dies this turn, you create a 2/2 white and blue Detective creature token. This makes it a pseudo-cantrip combat trick in decks that want both aggression and the Detective token type. Detective tokens are a MKM-specific creature type that appear across the set as a consequence-of-violence motif. Legal in Pauper — a notable format inclusion for a common instant. [Scryfall]

Art by Justine Cruz depicts a small cat-like creature erupting through a golden-orange burst, framed by two large foreground hands — the creature has broken free of a grip. The Neon Dynasty-era Kamigawa cat-creature associations aside, this is thematically unnamed in MKM. The large hands framing the scene may reference a captor or investigator unable to contain the feral creature — reinforcing the flavor-text theme of temper outrunning control. [Scryfall]

related_cards.name: Reckless Charge
related_cards.note: Classic red haste pump instant — functional predecessor to Felonious Rage's core effect

related_cards.name: Thrill of Possibility
related_cards.note: Red instant with conditional value, comparable design philosophy

related_cards.name: Alquist Proft, Master Sleuth
related_cards.note: Creates the same white-blue Detective token type that Felonious Rage spawns on creature death
